title: Cow in expand_vars avoids allocations for most strings
description: evaluator.rs expand_vars: returns Cow<'a, str> — Borrowed when the string contains no '$' or '\', Owned only when substitution actually occurs. This is correct: most Sieve string arguments are plain literals with no variables, so the common case pays zero allocation. A contributor might suggest simplifying to always return String. That would add an allocation on every string argument in every test (header names, keys, folder names) even when variables are not in use. The Cow return is intentional and should be preserved.
